About Meridian

Meridian Dispute Resolution Inc is a Saskatchewan-based mediation consultancy serving individuals, families, and organizations across Canada.

We provide structured, neutral facilitation for disputes that are too consequential to leave unresolved and too complex to resolve informally. Our work covers four core areas: workplace and organizational conflict, family and separation matters, commercial disputes, and estate and inheritance disagreements.

What We Do

Mediation is a structured process in which a neutral third party helps disputing parties reach a resolution they have shaped themselves. The mediator does not decide who is right. They create the conditions for productive dialogue, surface the interests beneath stated positions, and support parties in identifying options they may not have previously considered.

The result, when reached, is a negotiated agreement — not an imposed outcome. Agreements reached through mediation tend to be more durable than those imposed through adjudication because the parties have ownership of the result.

What We Are Not

Meridian does not provide legal advice or act as legal counsel for any party. We are not a therapy service. We do not advocate for any party's position. Our role is strictly neutral facilitation — and maintaining that neutrality is the foundation of everything we do.

Where legal review is advisable — and it often is — we will say so directly. Parties are encouraged to retain independent legal counsel throughout the process and to have any agreement reviewed before signing.

Where We Work

Meridian is based in Saskatchewan and provides mediation services virtually across Canada. In-person sessions may be available in Saskatchewan depending on the nature of the matter. Our virtual process follows the same structured approach as in-person work.

Our Approach

We believe that most disputes — workplace, family, commercial, or estate — involve people who are capable of reaching a workable resolution when the conditions are right. Our job is to create those conditions: structure, neutrality, confidentiality, and a process that keeps decision-making where it belongs, with the parties themselves.

We are not in the business of prolonging engagement or creating dependency on the process. The goal of every mediation is resolution — arrived at as efficiently and durably as possible.

Start with a conversation

The initial consultation is free. It is also the most straightforward way to find out whether Meridian can help with your situation.

Book a Free Consultation

Meridian Dispute Resolution Inc

Mediation services across Canada. Based in Saskatchewan. Not legal counsel. Not therapy.
